description: Tracks progress of the recurring quote-sourcing audit so each run covers the next unaudited batch instead of re-checking randomly. Order is the alphabetically sorted list of collections/*.json filenames (without extension); within a file, quotes are walked in their existing array order.

cursor.note: quoteIndex is the 0-based index of the NEXT quote to audit within `file`. Next run starts at bhagavad-gita quotes[5] (gita-006).

runs.resolution: fixed in this run's PR; content corrected to full Season 1 title-card text
runs.reportedIn: see GitHub issue created this run

runs.issue: minor misquote (dropped the word 'but'); two other candidates initially flagged by first-pass research were re-verified and confirmed correct, not changed
runs.resolution: fixed in this run's PR; content corrected to full line per closed-caption archive of the S1E4 clip
runs.reportedIn: see GitHub issue created this run
runs.processNote: discovered 'michael-jordan' collection (52 quotes, added 2026-07-08) was missing from the order[] list and would never have been audited; added it to order[] and totalQuotes

runs.issue: bsg-023: wrong episode cited (source said S3E1 'Occupation'; line is actually from S3E4 'Exodus, Part 2'). bsg-025: dropped leading word 'No,' from Starbuck's line. A third candidate, bsg-024, was flagged by an initial pass for possibly dropping the word 'our' before 'democracy', but could not be confirmed against a primary transcript (episode transcript sites returned 403s; only secondary paraphrases were available) -- left unchanged and noted as needing manual re-check.
runs.resolution: bsg-023 and bsg-025 fixed in this run's PR; battlestar-galactica finishes this pass (25/25 quotes now audited). gita-001..gita-005 all passed with no issues (Edwin Arnold's public-domain 'Song Celestial' translation, verse-cited -- a strong primary source).
runs.reportedIn: see GitHub issue created this run
runs.unresolvedNote: bsg-024 needs a follow-up manual check against a primary transcript for the possible missing word 'our' -- not fixed this run due to insufficient confidence.
